local_server.description: Local Context7 MCP server using @upstash/context7-mcp package
pros: No external dependencies on Context7 servers
Full control over the MCP server process
cons: Need to manage the server process
local_server.use_case: Development, testing, and when you want local control

remote_server.description: Remote Context7 MCP server using HTTP connection
remote_server.use_case: Production environments and when you want simplicity

recommendations.development: Use local_server for better debugging and control
recommendations.testing: Use local_server for consistent testing environment
recommendations.production: Use remote_server for simplicity and reliability
recommendations.hybrid: Enable both and switch based on needs

local_setup: 2. The @upstash/context7-mcp package will be installed automatically via npx
3. Use the local_server configuration in your mcp.json
4. Start your MCP client with the local configuration
remote_setup: 2. Use the remote_server configuration in your mcp.json
3. Ensure your API key is correct
4. Start your MCP client with the remote configuration

api_credentials.note: API key is already configured in all environment configurations
